rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HAHN ET AL VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant General Electric Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 14, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant General Electric Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Hahn's deposition testimony and declaration, the Form 108 and Form 110 documents, and the deposition testimony of Mr. Laughter create a triable issue whether Mr. Hahn was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant (gasket material in GE old style acorn streetlights between 1965-1974). This ruling moots plaintiffs' alternative request for denial pursuant to C.C.P. § 437c(h). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
